mnemonics.title: Waterfall Phases
mnemonics.content: R-D-I-T-D-M: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Deployment → Maintenance

mnemonics.title: Design Thinking Steps
mnemonics.content: E-D-I-P-T: Empathize → Define → Ideate → Prototype → Test

mnemonics.title: Agile Manifesto Values
mnemonics.content: I-C-P-O: Individuals and interactions over processes and tools, Working software over comprehensive documentation, Customer collaboration over contract negotiation, Responding to change over following a plan

acronyms.usage: The visual elements users interact with, including buttons, menus, and screens
The overall experience a user has with a product, including satisfaction and ease of use
Design methodology that prioritizes user needs throughout development
Product with core features for early user feedback
Architecture pattern separating data, UI, and logic
Complete process from requirements to maintenance
Criteria for considering a task complete in Agile
UX term for ending user interactions smoothly

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a waterfall flowing down in straight steps (Waterfall) versus a circle of continuous improvement (Agile iterations)

visualTips.visualize: Think of a restaurant: Model (Kitchen/data), View (Menu/display), Controller (Waiter/manager)
A non-linear journey with overlapping phases forming a flexible cycle
Two diamonds side by side: first diamond (problem space: Discover & Define), second diamond (solution space: Develop & Deliver)

analogies.analogy: Like sculpting - you gradually shape and refine the piece through multiple passes rather than creating it perfectly in one attempt
UI is the stage set (scenery, props), UX is the entire play experience (story, emotions, satisfaction)
Like borrowing money - quick fixes seem convenient now but accumulate interest through future rework costs
Like a skeleton restaurant - serves basic dishes to test the concept before adding full menu
